title: Was it Yuis intention to be absorbed by the Eva
display_title: Was it Yuis intention to be absorbed by the Eva
wikitext:
[]: Was it Yui's intention to be absorbed by the Eva?             Quote Post by Jobhug » Thu Feb 08, 2007 5:46 pm If so in which episode do we learn this?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Feb 08, 2007 5:47 pm 21' and 26'.

[]:             Quote Post by Jobhug » Thu Feb 08, 2007 5:52 pm Thanks, I'll admit that I don't have a great understanding of what happened pre Sachiel's attack. Off to whatch 21 and 26. EDIT: I whatched them, the way she talks to Fuyutsuki in 21' and Death does seem to imply that she anticipated the whole thing. It seems odd though that she says she's doing it for Shinji when as a result he spends the next 11 years in a miserable, isolated existance with no family. The scene at the lake in 26' that resembles 21' seems wierd though. She's telling Fuyutsuki about the Human will inside Eva that isn't there yet rather franky. From Evacommentary.org Reichu: This cut juxtaposes the present day (or whenever) with a conversation from 2003. It's kind of weird to hear Yui talking about her future self in the third person. The Japanese doesn't specify whether or not she does, actually, so the A2000A translation has Yui referring to herself. ("I will be lonely, but as long as I liveâ€¦ ") But that sounds weird, too, because she isn't the Eva yet when she says it. There is a way to make it work, by translating "sono naka ni yadoru, hito no kokoro to tomo ni" as "together with the human soul that will dwell within her" (since present tense can include "wills" unless specified otherwise). But, you know, that sounds weird, too, since while she's saying this, we see present-day Yui in her purple get-up with a flaming new 'do. I dunno. If anyone has any thoughts on this one, let me know. NEWER MORE BETTER EDIT: Through more browsing of these forums, the AnimeNation forums and Evacommentary.org I have come to the conclusion that Yui is far too involved in and perceptive of everything that goes on behind the scenes, and her plan was in motion before she got Absorbed. She's too much of a flawlessly nice "saint" character to have been following SEELE's plan when they are by my (and Fuyutsuki's) standards, selfish evil bastards.
